callRationale: Started with a broad freshness-bounded search for post-August-2025 TypeScript or JavaScript library release notes with API changes.
Narrowed to official React and GitHub sources after the first search surfaced React as a promising library release.
Scraped the exact official React 19.2 release post to capture the release date, npm availability, API names, behavioral descriptions, and code examples.
strengths: The final answer is grounded in the official React release post rather than third-party summaries.
Freshness controls were used with a start date of 2025-09-01.
The provider returned enough exact-page markdown to support concrete examples for more than three API changes.
weaknesses: The initial broad search returned noisy results from non-official and unrelated sources.
The GitHub changelog result was discovered but its focused chunks included older React releases, so the exact React blog scrape was necessary.
notableFailures: A first local harness call failed with ENOENT because the args JSON path was created relative to the wrong repository; this happened before any provider response and was corrected with an absolute evals/work path.
